title: Chapter 27 – Zhongnan Sect (2)
content: The Zhongnan disciples, momentarily stunned by that devastatingly swift swordwork, were gritting their teeth and reaching for their own blades when—

The bushes shuddered in unison with that jade-clear voice. A tremendous wave of true energy washed over them.

Rustle.

She descended like a celestial maiden, her presence overwhelming.

The realm of transcendence was here as well.

Jeong Yeon-shin stared at the woman who had landed between them like a mediator.

'She's strong.'

Her rare beauty did not even register in his eyes.

Her slender frame was enveloped in an imposing energy that belied her delicate build.

Just standing there, her extraordinary skill was palpable.

The guards bowed their heads at her gentle but firm words.

It had the quality of a senior correcting a junior's misstep.

They seemed to recognize their impropriety; their faces flushed.
The woman acknowledged their apologies with a nod, then turned to Jeong Yeon-shin and Hyeon Won-chang.

She moved her lips.

The leaf that grazed the hem of her flowing robe was split cleanly in two.

The energy of her entire being had taken on sharpness — a realm that seemed no lesser than Desolate Sect's blue level.

Hyeon Won-chang appeared completely overwhelmed.

He stood there gaping in stunned silence, so Jeong Yeon-shin spoke in his stead.

Her soft words carried an oppressive weight. Jeong Yeon-shin felt it distinctly.

It was not deliberately imbued with energy. It simply approached with an authority that was innately hers.

Only now did he truly grasp the power of the Nine Sects.

Hyeon Won-chang's exclamation confirmed her identity.

She had beheaded the leader of an evil sect's forces on the battlefield, so the stories went.

She was already a master whose name carried across the world.

The Sword Dragon, Wei Ji Myo-hwa.

Rumors held that she was a genius mistakenly sent down from heaven.

Mount Hua's Yu Hyeon was sixteen this year, the same as Jeong Yeon-shin, but if he were to undergo five years of grueling closed-door training, he might approach her level.

'Born and raised in Zhongnan, and already a great master at such a young age.'

Jeong Yeon-shin, at least four years her junior, had no inclination to measure himself against her.

Short-lived constitution. It had become habit to exclude his own age from such calculations.

Hyeon Won-chang offered his clasped fists.

Wei Ji Myo-hwa had scolded her own fellow disciples right in front of them.

How could one not respect that? Jeong Yeon-shin felt the same.

As if meeting a true Nine Sects martial artist he had only heard of in stories, he clasped his hands after Hyeon Won-chang.

He did not call her a female hero or lady but "great hero."

She was someone who had earned a dragon's title despite not being male.

A senior who roamed the martial world sword in hand, with incomparably rich experience and towering achievements.

Wei Ji Myo-hwa's gaze softened.

She was so beautiful that Hyeon Won-chang's earlier slack-jawed reaction became somewhat understandable.

Warmth colored her expression as she looked at Jeong Yeon-shin, and the way she represented the famed Zhongnan Sect was surprisingly natural.

She was the most striking presence he had encountered since Cheon Ju, the Sword Saint of Mount Hua, who was considered Ma Jin's rival.

Her bearing, he thought, was second to none.

As he raised his head from the greeting, a familiar face entered Jeong Yeon-shin's view.

His second brother, Jeong Jung-san, was standing there with a stunned expression.

* * *

He could not help but be surprised by Jeong Jung-san's words as they climbed the mountain together.

The Zhongnan Sword Immortal was said to be a swordsman surpassing Elder Yeo Il-sin — a figure of famous clan lineage.

He seldom descended to the secular world, yet his reputation was known throughout the land.

His niece Jeong Hye had been recognized for her talent.

Relief settled over him, but Jeong Jung-san was watching Jeong Yeon-shin with a complicated expression.

Jeong Yeon-shin remained sparing with his words, even before his brother.

Only the Desolate Sect Lord knew about his lifespan.

He had no desire to spread that burden any further.

Wei Ji Myo-hwa seemed to have taken a genuine liking to Jeong Yeon-shin.

Jeong Jung-san's expression shifted with her every word — the kind of discomfort that a Zhongnan disciple of his rank found difficult to witness.

'It isn't ambition. It's desperation.'

When he found it difficult to even muster courtesy for words that did not resonate, the group arrived at the Zhongnan Sect's mountain gate.

Behind Wei Ji Myo-hwa, who turned with a faint smile, spread an array of antique pavilions.

Compared to Desolate Sect it was modest, but the main mountain of the Nine Sects — where the power of tradition flowed directly — was something different altogether.

The pavilions, harmonized with the overwhelming mountain terrain, were extraordinary at first glance.

As she spoke, recalling the absolute being whose sword had annihilated the Jeong family, Jeong Yeon-shin and Hyeon Won-chang arrived at the Seeking Pavilion.

After climbing several rugged mountain peaks.

They happened upon an old man and a young girl emerging from the martial training ground beside them.

The girl's eyes went wide.

Perhaps because she had lost her parents overnight.

Jeong Yeon-shin caught the child as she ran to him, heartbroken, and held her close.

While he reunited with the sobbing Jeong Hye, Hyeon Won-chang, watching from the side, sniffled audibly.

After setting down the child — who refused to let go — he faced the old man who had been regarding him with a peculiar gaze.

Wei Ji Myo-hwa said. Her tone was the one she might use with a doting grandfather.

After Jeong Yeon-shin and Hyeon Won-chang clasped their fists and introduced themselves, the old man, hands behind his back, slowly spoke.

Hyeon Won-chang, who had been wearing an expression of mounting disbelief, shouted in something close to a scream.

The way he immediately clapped his own hand over his mouth was rather childish, but no one in the group faulted him for it.

Jeong Yeon-shin calmly clasped his fists once more.

He had observed the Desolate Sect Lord from a distance.

While he felt reverence for the old man's reputation, it did not strike him with the same thunderbolt of shock it gave Hyeon Won-chang.

They exchanged brief words.

He explained that they had climbed Zhongnan Mountain officially to observe the impact on civilians caught between warring sects, and privately to confirm the safety of his blood relatives.

Jong Yeo-il, the Zhongnan Sword Immortal, studied Jeong Yeon-shin in silence. Then, abruptly:

Regardless of Jeong Yeon-shin's inner bewilderment, the Zhongnan Sword Immortal raised his hand and pointed to one side.

It was a place that had already been nagging at Jeong Yeon-shin's attention.

Various lines were etched across the rough surface of a cliff, half-veiled in faint mist.
Wei Ji Myo-hwa called to him, her expression uncomprehending.
She drew her fine brows together, unable to understand. The Zhongnan Sword Immortal let out a laugh.
His tone was unyielding. Everyone present could feel the ferocity of his final obsession.

Following the Zhongnan Sword Immortal's gesture, Wei Ji Myo-hwa's eyes widened.

Jeong Yeon-shin had already gone to the mountain peak's edge and was staring at the opposite cliff with his neck craned forward.

Hyeon Won-chang hovered behind him, poised to grab his collar the instant he leaned too far, fidgeting with anxiety.

He was clearly in a state of total absorption. Anyone could see it.

'Sword traces.'

Jeong Yeon-shin thought. The multiple trajectories carved into the cliff contained a keen sharpness.

They looked like overwhelming sword marks that had survived even the erosion of time.

Had they been left by the Sword Immortal?

The sword traces that ran the length of the cliff did not feel like human handiwork at all.

They would have been impossible to make unless one could walk on empty air.
the Zhongnan Sword Immortal said, having appeared beside him. Hyeon Won-chang flinched at his sudden proximity.
Jeong Yeon-shin asked, his gaze still locked on the cliff.

This meant he had been an absolute master of unprecedented strength.

The Zhongnan Sword Immortal spoke of his regret.

An old swordsman standing at the end of his life, scattering everything he had lived for into futility.

He said only the Sword Master's sword remained. He did not take offense at the Desolate Sect Lord being called a demon.
Jeong Yeon-shin asked. The Zhongnan Sword Immortal nodded without hesitation.
The Zhongnan Sword Immortal regarded Jeong Yeon-shin with a strange expression and pointed to the cliff.

Jeong Yeon-shin followed the old man's finger with his eyes and furrowed his brow slightly.

To see it more clearly.

There was a phrase written in a single stroke within the cliff's mist.

'Who is worthy.' The worthy shall obtain it.

Only then did Jeong Yeon-shin tear his eyes from the cliff and turn his head.

Wei Ji Myo-hwa wore an inscrutable expression. She — the one called the Zhongnan Sect's unprecedented genius — did not deny the weight.

But that was all. Their gazes met briefly, and she still looked unconvinced.

She could not fathom the situation itself, beyond any personal reservations.

Jeong Yeon-shin thought that perhaps this Zhongnan Sword Immortal might be a master surpassing even his maternal grandfather Ma Yeon-jeok.

Because until now, only the Desolate Sect Lord had ever seen through his true nature.

'Even with such a master, they can't defeat the Tyrant Sword Sect decisively?'

Whether Ma Yeon-jeok's martial arts had dulled from his prime, or whether the Zhongnan Sword Immortal stood beyond the assessments of common martial enthusiasts.

Either way, he was simply grateful for yet another opportunity to grow stronger.

It was while he was stroking Hye's head — she had come running and was gazing up at him — that the question came.
the Zhongnan Sword Immortal asked. Jeong Yeon-shin silently drew his pure white Desolate Sword.
